mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 50% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index indicate immediate supply-side bottlenecks that force corporate margin compression and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not materialize following industrial production declines exceeding 0.5% during post-shock recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate creates a real rate tightening environment that increases equity valuation multiples compression and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.3% without a concurrent funds rate cut.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases household income and consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over three months.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.01, or 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months with positive payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts decline exceeding 10% signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and related consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in at least 60% of months where housing starts drop more than 10% during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production expansion during periods of high unemployment indicates an overheating supply response that precedes central bank intervention, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1.0% while UNRATE remains above 5.0%
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising industrial production and elevated unemployment fails to predict negative 5-day returns in over 55% of observed instances.
